what_it_does: Documents a tentative design for a Decision-Sequence Surface: a narrated slide sequence of decision cards, with render wiring, host/write contracts, mode-colour behavior, V-surface/subsurface handling, build/reuse split, punch-list items, B-phase card set, and open implementation questions.
observations: Frames the surface as a guided decision walk where each decision is resolved, rendered, narrated, decided, written back, and re-resolved.
Names RUNG C as the missing render join: binding resolved address data into archetype slots instead of hardcoded specimens.
Specifies exact runtime contract details for decision rendering and writing, including `decision:rendered`, `decision_take`, option label values, and bare decision addresses for takes.
Separates reuse items from named build items, including render binding, mode-to-token wiring, decision schemas, RHM narration/write-back, and story-to-unit drift repair.
Treats mode colour as mostly present but needing a wire from mode state into cascade colour tokens.
Describes the V as surface, mode-setter, and RHM handle, with sub-surfaces intended to use a connection model rather than placeholder panel/window stubs.
Adds host-contract, visual punch-list, responsive/generative form-factor rules, and B-phase seven-card scope directly into the design note.
Ends with concrete open questions for Tim about first render target, colour breadth, DNA-composition seam, story-unit repair, and sequence shape.
standouts: The file is both concept design and implementation contract, with exact event names, field names, address shapes, and failure modes.
It repeatedly pushes rendering toward resolved records, archetypes, viewport scope, and generation rules rather than per-card patches.
The rerank-loadout visible-degrade note makes degraded grounding a rendered decision consequence rather than hidden system state.
conventions: Uses numbered sections with later inserted subsections such as 6b-6f.
Combines prose, tables, bullets, code blocks, quoted user language, commit references, and path references in one design ledger entry.
Marks uncertain or delegated decisions explicitly with OPEN, fork, gated, and Tim's call language.
Uses strong emphasis and exact-string warnings for contract-sensitive details.
Breaks a pure design-note convention by embedding implementation punch-lists and host integration checklists in the same file.
concerns: The section numbering repeats `## 6` before introducing 6b-6f, which may make references awkward.
The document mixes future design, verified claims, open questions, and live contract details, so status boundaries require careful reading.
Several claims depend on referenced files, commits, or external checklists that are not included in this file.
notes: Brief someone that this is the decision-sequence surface design ledger plus its render/write/host contract and pending build questions.
questions: From this file alone, which open fork choices has Tim already answered, if any?
From this file alone, which parts are already implemented versus only asserted as verified elsewhere?
Where is the long-term store for generated decision instances meant to live?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the file: it is a tentative correction-oriented design artifact, and it also accumulates concrete integration contracts, punch-list items, and phase scope.
apparent_themes: explicit contracts to prevent silent failures
RHM narration through interactive decision flow
intention_signals: It seems intended to align multiple substrates around one decision-card sequence surface while preserving exact render, write-back, host, and review constraints for later implementation.
novelty: A decision workflow is expressed as a narrated slide sequence whose UI state, decision state, colour, and grounding notes are all meant to resolve from addressed records and marks.
contribution: Contributes a neutral design-and-contract layer for keeping decision sequence rendering, narration, choice capture, mode colour, host events, write-back semantics, form-factor generation, and visible degradation together in one file-local specification.
summary_for_embedding: A tentative design and integration contract for a Decision-Sequence Surface that renders addressed decision records as narrated interactive slide cards with exact host/write semantics, mode-colour wiring, generative form-factor goals, and B-phase scope.
intention_for_embedding: This was for giving Tim a correctable, implementation-oriented surface design that unifies decision cards, RHM narration, render binding, mark-based write-back, and compositional generation rules.
